hero.question: Chicago White Sox vs. Kansas City Royals
hero.prob_take: The White Sox are priced as 2.3-to-1 favorites at 69.5%, but the +30.5 point surge in 24 hours screams sharp money flooding in on Kansas City rather than genuine edge in Chicago's direction. The Royals have been quietly competitive lately while the Sox rotation is a mess, so a 30% underdog price actually looks like it's catching the real probability gap.

daily_take.headline: White Sox surge 30 points in 24 hours, but the line still smells like easy money for contrarians
daily_take.deck: The White Sox jumped to 69.5% odds overnight on what looks like standard MLB shuffle. But this is a team that lost 121 games last year and hasn't meaningfully improved. The market overweighted recency and fresh action, not fundamentals. Sharps should look hard at the Royals value.

sidebar.headline: Peace deal odds cratered 11 points as Iran tensions escalate, but 7.5% still prices in way too much optimism for April 2026 diplomatic miracles.

sidebar.headline: Warriors jumped 13 points despite playing at home as favorites, suggesting casual money piled in rather than any meaningful edge shift.

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic fell 18 points to 14.5%, the real mover. Regional escalation made April normalcy a long shot, but the market may have overshot the downside.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal Traffic: 14.5%
